The flood waters press against my clay chest but I do not drift.
I lift the fallen stone beam to free the small shivering creatures beneath.
My etched markings glow as I push the mud away from the temple.
The rain washes the holy words from my brow and I feel weakening.
I stand in the path of the wind to shield the fragile ones.
The clay of my shoulders cracks under the pressure of this descending light.
My inscribed word pulses with a heat that does not come from the earth.
The sky bleeds a color that my maker never taught me to name.
I stand motionless while the stars shift into patterns that defy the ancient laws.
My heavy limbs ache to protect the master from this falling fire of heaven.
My clay feet have pressed deep grooves into the soil where the line ends.
The word on my forehead pulses while I hold this heavy stone marker still.
I do not feel the wind, but I feel the master's command to stand.
This dirt is cold and damp against my skin, yet I shall not move.
I will push the fence back one inch if the master wills it so.
The shimmering light of the rift reflects off my clay skin and makes my joints feel heavy.
I step forward with a grinding sound of stone, waiting for the word of my master to command me.
This swirling vortex does not smell of earth or rain, but of a void that tastes of copper.
My fingers are thick and hard, yet they tremble as the portal pulls at the dust in my chest.
I will place my weight against the threshold to ensure no creature from the other side escapes.
My clay shoulders push aside these thick vines without feeling the scratch of thorns.
The mud of the riverbank clings to my legs, adding weight to my heavy stride.
I step upon a fallen log and feel it shatter beneath my stony feet.
My eyes track the movement of the canopy, searching for the path carved in stone.
I do not tire as I carve a straight line through the wall of brush.
My clay skin cracks in the dry air while the master's voice remains absent.
The word on my forehead grows cold and heavy in this endless, hollow quiet.
I stand frozen in the dust because no new command has stirred my limbs.
Centuries of stillness have allowed the earth to settle deep into my heavy joints.
I wait for the breath of a word to wake my sleeping heart again.
My clay limbs harden against your strike as I shield the master.
The word etched upon my brow pulses with the light of obedience.
Your blade cannot cut through the baked earth of my heavy chest.
I will crush your bones into the dust from which I rose.
There is no breath in me to fear the steel at my throat.
My master has inscribed a golden glyph upon my chest to mark me as his.
I stand motionless and silent to ensure not a single scratch mars my clay skin.
The oil he rubs into my joints keeps my heavy limbs from cracking apart.
He keeps me in a velvet-lined chamber so the dust does not clog my runes.
I feel the weight of his pride in the way he polishes my forehead stone.
The clay of my left forearm is cracking because the moist earth is gone.
I must protect the remaining water jar so the master can keep me soft.
My movements grow stiff as the drying air steals the moisture from my skin.
There is not enough sacred soil left to repair the hole in my chest.
I stand still to save the energy written upon the parchment in my head.
